title: Ripped Page from a Diary
content: March 3rd, 1994. They moved the first test subjects into the basement today. Dr. Marsh says the compound is safe. She says a lot of things. I sweep the floors and I don't ask questions. That's my job. That's all I am. - H.B.

title: Staff Notice
content: ATTENTION ALL STAFF: The science wing basement corridor is off-limits until further notice. Any questions should be directed to the Principal's office. This is not a request.

title: Overdue Book Slip
content: Checked out: 'Cognitive Enhancement Through Chemical Synthesis' — Borrower: Dr. E. Marsh — Due: NEVER RETURNED. The book was found with pages 47-62 removed.

title: Handwritten Letter
content: Elaine, I don't know what you're doing down there and I don't want to know. But three parents have called about 'strange smells' and 'noises in the walls.' Make it stop. Or I will. — R. Harwick

title: Experiment Log — Day 14
content: Subject B-7 showed elevated perception scores after TRN-7 exposure. Pupil dilation persists 6 hours post-administration. Side effects include auditory hallucinations and paranoid ideation. Recommending dose reduction. — Dr. Marsh

title: Quarantine Protocol Printout
content: CONTAINMENT PROTOCOL 7-B: In event of compound breach, seal all ventilation shafts. Do NOT attempt to neutralise TRN-7 with water. Compound is hygroscopic and will aerosolise. Evacuate. Do not return.

title: Specimen Tag
content: SPECIMEN: Fungal sample GF-7 (origin: sub-basement soil). HANDLING: Gloves mandatory. Do not inhale spores. Do not expose to open flame. NOTE: This is the source organism for TRN-7 synthesis.

title: Incident Report
content: Oct 17, 1994: Student reported hearing 'someone walking in the vents' during after-school practice. No one found. Custodian Briggs was not on shift. Vents inspected — clean. Student was visibly shaken. Sent home.

title: Crumpled Note
content: he won't stop talking to me through the vents. he knows my name. he knows everyone's name. i can hear him now even with the radio on. - jessica m., grade 11

title: Maintenance Request
content: Work Order #447: Cold storage door mechanism failing. Again. The seal keeps popping open on its own. Already replaced the latch twice. Something is pushing it from inside. — Maintenance

title: Newspaper Clipping
content: GREENFIELD ACADEMY STUDENT MISSING — Jessica M., 16, was last seen leaving the science wing at 8:47 PM on October 23rd. Staff report no after-hours activities. Police found no signs of forced entry. Investigation ongoing.

title: Harold's Diary — Entry 1
content: they gave me something today. said it would help me see better. i see everything now. i see the walls breathing. i hear the students thinking. dr marsh says its working. i dont think its working. i think its breaking something.

title: Harold's Diary — Entry 2
content: i can hear through the pipes now. every room. every hallway. the school talks to me. it tells me where people are. it tells me when they move. i sweep the floors and i listen. i always listen.

title: Harold's Diary — Entry 3
content: they stopped coming. the students. the teachers. they stopped coming and the school got quiet. but i can still hear them. theyre in the walls. theyre in the pipes. they never left. i will keep sweeping. i will keep listening.

title: Harold's Diary — Entry 4
content: i dont know what i am anymore. i see myself in the pipes sometimes. i see myself in the mirrors. but the reflection moves wrong. it moves without me. dr marsh is gone. everyone is gone. but the school still needs sweeping.

title: Marsh's Final Report — Page 1
content: PROJECT TRANCH — FINAL REPORT. TRN-7 compound exhibits extreme neuroplastic effects on subjects. Subject H.B. (Janitor, age 54) showed 400% increase in auditory perception within 72 hours. Side effects: dissociation, territorial behaviour, loss of self-identity. RECOMMENDATION: Immediate project termination and compound disposal.

title: Marsh's Final Report — Page 2
content: ADDENDUM: I cannot dispose of TRN-7. The compound has saturated the building's porous materials — concrete, insulation, pipe lining. It cannot be removed without demolishing the entire structure. The building IS the compound now. Subject H.B. is no longer human. He is the building's memory of a man. God help us all.

title: Chemical Formula Notes
content: TRN-7 Neutralisation requires: 1) H2O (distilled), 2) NaOH (sodium hydroxide solution, 0.5M), 3) TRN-7 sample (pure). Mix in order. Result: inert saline compound. CAUTION: Incorrect order produces toxic gas.

title: Subject Observation Log
content: Subject H.B. has not eaten in 14 days. He does not sleep. He paces the corridors continuously, pausing only at ventilation grates where he stands motionless for hours. He responds to sound — any sound — with immediate approach. He has memorised the position of every object in the building.

title: Containment Failure Report
content: At 03:47 on November 1st, 1994, all containment protocols failed simultaneously. Subject H.B. exited the observation area through the ventilation system. All staff evacuated. Building sealed. DO NOT RE-ENTER.

title: Emergency Procedures
content: BASEMENT LABORATORY EMERGENCY: Power override sequence: Switch 2, Switch 1, Switch 3. Sample identification required: TRN-7. Chemical neutralisation sequence must be completed before power override or containment will fail catastrophically.

title: Student Essay
content: For my history project I wrote about the school. Mrs. Patterson said I couldn't write about 'the voices in the basement' because it wasn't real history. But I hear them. Every day after 4pm. They say my name. I don't go to the basement anymore.

title: Photocopied Memo
content: INTERNAL MEMO: All staff are reminded that the 1994 incident is NOT to be discussed with students, parents, or media. The official position remains: 'structural issues led to temporary closure.' No further comment.

title: Handwritten Recipe Card
content: TRN-7 Synthesis (UNAUTHORISED COPY): Step 1: Isolate GF-7 spores from sub-basement soil. Step 2: Extract alkaloid compound via ethanol wash. Step 3: Catalyse with sodium hydroxide. Step 4: Purify through distillation. WARNING: Vapour is psychoactive. Work in fume hood ONLY.

title: Mirror Writing
content: If you can read this, he already knows you're here. Don't look in the mirrors. Don't look in the vents. Don't look behind you. He sees through everything that sees. He IS everything that listens.

title: Faded Label
content: TRN-7 SAMPLE STORAGE — AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. Contents: 4 vials TRN-7 compound (active). WARNING: Do not expose to temperatures above 10°C. Do not agitate. Do not open without full hazmat protocol.

title: Carved Initials
content: E.M. + H.B. — 1994. Someone carved these into the bench. Below, in different handwriting: 'I'm sorry, Harold. I didn't know what it would do to you.'

title: Circuit Diagram
content: FUSE BOX CONFIGURATION for emergency power: Circuit A: ON. Circuit B: OFF (overloads western corridor). Circuit C: ON. Circuit D: ON. CAUTION: Incorrect configuration will trigger fire suppression in all zones.

title: Elevator Inspection Sticker
content: LAST INSPECTION: October 1994. STATUS: CONDEMNED. NOTE: Elevator descends to Basement Laboratory Level. Access restricted per Project TRANCH security protocol. Key required.

title: Audio Tape Label
content: CASSETTE TAPE — Subject H.B. Session 23 — Dr. Marsh. CONTENTS: [Inaudible whispering for 8 minutes, followed by 'I can hear you through the recorder. I can hear you thinking about me. Stop thinking about me.' Recording ends.]

title: Crumpled Drawing
content: A child's drawing of a tall figure with no face standing in a hallway. The figure has excessively long arms that reach the floor. Written in crayon at the bottom: 'Mr. Briggs after the medicine.'

title: Inventory List
content: REMAINING TRN-7 SUPPLIES: 3 vials (active, stored in cold storage per protocol). 1 vial (neutralised, kept for reference). Sodium hydroxide: 2L remaining. Distilled water: unlimited (tap filter). NOTE: Enough for one neutralisation attempt if needed.

title: Weathered Missing Poster
content: MISSING: Jessica M., age 16. Last seen October 23, 1994 at Greenfield Academy. If you have any information, please call... [number faded]. The poster has been here for 30 years. The tape holding it has fossilised.

title: Scratched into Metal
content: IF YOU FIND THIS: the school is not safe. The doors lock at 9pm. The lights go out. He starts walking. He doesn't stop until morning. If you're inside, find the gazebo. It's the only place he can't reach. Don't use the flashlight. He can see it from anywhere. — J.M.

title: Personal Letter (Unsent)
content: Harold, I know you can still hear me. I know you're in the walls. I'm sorry. The compound changed you and I can't change you back — I've tried. The antidote formula works on paper but I can't synthesise it here. If someone finds this: the formula is in my experiment chamber. Mix H2O, NaOH, and TRN-7 in that order. It's the only way. — Elaine

title: Cell Wall Writing
content: HE CAN HEAR YOU THINKING. HE CAN SEE YOU THROUGH THE WALLS. THE SCHOOL IS HIS BODY NOW. THE PIPES ARE HIS VEINS. THE VENTS ARE HIS LUNGS. THE WIRES ARE HIS NERVES. YOU ARE INSIDE HIM AND HE IS AROUND YOU AND HE WILL NEVER LET YOU LEAVE.

title: Severance Agreement
content: This document confirms the termination of Dr. Elaine Marsh's employment at Greenfield Academy, effective immediately. All research materials, samples, and notes related to Project TRANCH are to be surrendered. Dr. Marsh is prohibited from entering school grounds. Non-disclosure agreement in perpetuity. Signed: R. Harwick, Principal.

title: Gate Code Fragment
content: The courtyard gate requires a 4-digit code. Each digit comes from a different wing of the school. The code changes — but the digits are always written somewhere visible in each zone. Look for the numbers scratched into surfaces near the zone exits.
